input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
## .NET MAUI Development Setup on macOS

### 1. Install .NET SDK

- Download from [dot.net](https://dot.net/download) — get the **latest .NET 8 or .NET 9 SDK** (MAUI ships with both).
- Alternatively, install via Homebrew: `brew install --cask dotnet-sdk`
- Verify: `dotnet --list-sdks`

### 2. Install Xcode (for iOS)

- Install from the **Mac App Store** (ensures you get the Apple-signed version).
- After install, run:
  ```
  xcode-select --install          # command-line tools
  sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer
  ```
- Accept the license: `sudo xcodebuild -license accept`
- Verify: `xcodebuild -version`

### 3. Install the .NET MAUI Workload

```bash
sudo dotnet workload install maui
```

- Discover available workloads: `dotnet workload search`
- Check what's installed: `dotnet workload list`
- This pulls in the iOS, Android, and platform-specific SDKs automatically.

### 4. Android SDK & JDK (auto-managed)

The MAUI workload installs the **Android SDK** and a compatible **JDK** automatically. If you need manual control:

- Install Android Studio from [developer.android.com](https://developer.android.com/studio) for the emulator and SDK Manager.
- The MAUI build discovers the SDK via `ANDROID_HOME`. Default location: `~/Library/Developer/Xamarin/android-sdk-macosx`
- Check what MAUI expects: `dotnet workload list` and inspect the manifest in `<sdk-path>/sdk-manifests/`.

### 5. IDE (pick one)

| IDE | Source |
|-----|--------|
| **Visual Studio Code** + C# Dev Kit + .NET MAUI extension | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com) |
| **JetBrains Rider** | [jetbrains.com/rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) |

> Visual Studio for Mac was retired in Aug 2024. VS Code with the MAUI extension is now the official path on macOS.

### 6. Verify Everything

```bash
# 1 — SDK and workloads
dotnet --list-sdks
dotnet workload list          # should show "maui" (and maui-ios, maui-android)

# 2 — Create and build a test app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net8.0-android   # (or net9.0-android)

# Build for iOS
dotnet build -f net8.0-ios       # requires Xcode

# 3 — Run on simulators
dotnet build -f net8.0-ios -t:Run
dotnet build -f net8.0-android -t:Run

# 4 — One-stop diagnostic
dotnet tool install -g redth.net.maui.check
maui-check                       # audits all dependencies
```

### 7. Version Discovery Cheat Sheet

| Question | Command |
|----------|---------|
| Which .NET SDKs do I have? | `dotnet --list-sdks` |
| Which MAUI workload version? | `dotnet workload list` |
| Which Xcode/iOS SDK? | `xcodebuild -version && xcrun --show-sdk-version` |
| Which Android SDK/JDK? | Open Android Studio → SDK Manager, or `ls $ANDROID_HOME/platforms/` |
| Are all deps satisfied? | `maui-check` |

### Key Gotchas

- **Xcode version matters** — each .NET MAUI release supports specific Xcode versions. Check the [MAUI release notes](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/whats-new/) for the compatibility matrix.
- **Apple Silicon** — the .NET SDK is ARM-native; make sure you grab the **Arm64** installer from dot.net.
- Run `dotnet workload update` periodically to stay current.
